sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: TransUnion Applauds FHFA, HUD for Advancing VantageScore in Mortgage Lending
feed.summary: TransUnion applauds the FHFA and HUD for moving forward with the inclusion of VantageScore 4.0 in the U.S. mortgage market, aiming to expand access to affordable homeownership. The company emphasizes its commitment to driving a smooth transition, offering competitive pricing, multi-year pricing forecasts, and a free credit score simulator. TransUnion highlights that VantageScore 4.0, powered by its trended credit data, will enhance predictive power and provide a more comprehensive view of borrower credit performance.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.1875 per Share, Payable June 4 to Holders of Record May 14
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) has announced that it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.1875 per share. The dividend is payable on June 4 to shareholders of record as of May 14. This decision was published on MarketScreener, indicating the company's consistent return of capital to its investors.

feed.title: VeriSign (VRSN) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: VeriSign reported strong Q1 2026 results, reaching a record high in .com and .net domain names due to strong new registrations and improved renewal rates. The company raised its 2026 revenue and domain growth guidance and announced a $0.71 increase in the .com wholesale price, effective November 1, 2026. Management also discussed its continued commitment to 100% service availability and its cautious evaluation of participating in ICANN's new gTLD application round.

feed.title: Parker Hannifin raises quarterly dividend 11% to $2.00 per share By Investing.com
feed.summary: Parker Hannifin Corporation announced an 11% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $2.00 per share, marking the 304th consecutive quarterly dividend. The company boasts a 70-year streak of annual dividend increases, placing it among the S&P 500's top dividend-growth records. This decision reflects the Board's confidence in Parker Hannifin's consistent cash flow and long-term outlook, alongside recent strong financial results including exceeding Q2 2026 earnings and revenue forecasts.

feed.title: Parker-Hannifin raises quarterly dividend by 11.1% to $2.00/share
feed.summary: Parker-Hannifin (PH) has announced an 11.1% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it from $1.80 per share to $2.00 per share. This dividend is payable on March 1, 2024, to shareholders of record as of February 9, 2024. The company maintains a long history of consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs : Announces Redemption of All Outstanding Depositary Shares Representing Interests in Its 3.80% Fixed-Rate Reset Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series T
feed.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. announced its intention to redeem all outstanding depositary shares representing interests in its 3.80% Fixed-Rate Reset Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series T, on May 10, 2026. The redemption price will be $1,000 per depositary share, plus any declared and unpaid dividends. Dividends on the Series T Preferred Stock will cease to accrue after the redemption date.

feed.title: Generac power systems is recalling generators for a fire risk
feed.summary: Generac Power Systems has recalled certain orange and black generators due to a fire risk. The company is offering free repairs for the affected units. This recall aims to address the safety hazard and ensure the generators are safe for use.

feed.title: Altria Group (NYSE:MO) Shares Up 3.1% - What's Next?
feed.summary: Altria Group (NYSE:MO) shares rose 3.1% on Thursday, trading around $67.17. Analysts have a mixed view, with a consensus "Hold" rating and a target price of $65.88, though some firms increased their targets. The company recently declared a quarterly dividend of $1.06, resulting in a 6.3% yield, but reported a slight miss on EPS and a 0.5% decrease in revenue year-over-year in its latest quarter.

feed.title: Caterpillar Inc (CAT) Stock Up 3.3% but GF Value Says Overvalued -- GF Score: 84/100
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc (CAT) saw its shares rise 3.3% to $835.24, yet GuruFocus's GF Value™ indicates the stock is significantly overvalued by 117.7% compared to its intrinsic value of $383.64. Despite a strong GF Score™ of 84/100, driven by high profitability and growth, the valuation score is very low (1/10), and insiders have sold $99.5 million worth of shares, suggesting caution for potential investors.

feed.title: Parker-Hannifin Raises Quarterly Dividend 11% to $2.00 per Share, Payable June 5 to Holders of Record May 8
feed.summary: Parker-Hannifin Corporation announced an 11% increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it to $2.00 per share. This dividend will be payable on June 5, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 8, 2026. This move indicates the company's confidence in its financial performance and commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

feed.title: AMG Schedules Conference Call to Discuss Q1 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: AMG (NYSE: AMG) will release its Q1 2026 financial results on May 1, 2026, followed by a conference call at 8:00 a.m. ET. President and CEO Jay C. Horgen and CFO Dava E. Ritchea will host the call to discuss quarterly results and future outlook. The article also provides details on recent insider trading, revenue figures, hedge fund activity, and analyst ratings for AMG.

feed.title: Intel Corporation 2026 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NASDAQ:INTC) 2026-04-23
feed.summary: This article provides the earnings call presentation slideshow published by Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) for their 2026 Q1 results. The presentation was released in conjunction with their earnings call on April 23, 2026. SA Transcripts, a team focused on publishing quarterly earnings calls, wrote the article.

feed.title: Jabil declares $0.08 dividend
feed.summary: Jabil (NYSE: JBL) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.08 per share. This dividend is payable on June 3, 2024, to shareholders of record as of May 17, 2024. The ex-dividend date for this payment is May 16, 2024.

feed.title: Ingersoll-Rand declares $0.02 dividend
feed.summary: Ingersoll-Rand (NYSE: IR) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.02 per share. The dividend is payable on June 13, 2024, to shareholders of record as of May 1, 2024.

feed.title: Memphis' largest company searches for third CFO since 2020
feed.summary: FedEx, Memphis' largest company, is initiating an internal and external search for its next chief financial officer, marking the third CFO search since 2020. This comes as John Dietrich departs the position. The company has recently been involved in other significant business activities, including a branding deal for FedEx Freight and job cuts.

feed.title: A Look at CSX Corp (CSX) After 7.0% Gain -- GF Value $34.75 vs Price $46.18
feed.summary: CSX Corp (CSX) shares increased by 7.0%, bringing its year-to-date gain to 27.8%. Despite strong momentum, the stock is considered significantly overvalued by GuruFocus, trading at a 32.9% premium to its GF Value estimate of $34.75. Insider selling also outweighs buying, suggesting potential caution from those closest to the company.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems Inc (CDNS) Stock Down 5.2% -- Now Underva
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems Inc (CDNS) shares dropped 5.2% and are currently priced at $314.33, which is 13.4% below its GF Value™ of $362.90, indicating undervaluation. The company boasts a strong GF Score™ of 99/100, driven by excellent profitability, growth, and valuation ratings. However, insider selling totaling $7.6 million over the last three months raises concerns despite the overall positive financial metrics.

feed.title: Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) CTO sells 43,224 shares, converts Class B stock
feed.summary: Datadog's Chief Technology Officer, Alexis Le-Quoc, sold 43,224 shares of Class A Common Stock on April 22, 2026, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, with weighted-average prices between $130.27 and $133.16 per share. Concurrently, he converted 43,224 shares of Class B Common Stock into Class A shares at no cost. Le-Quoc retains substantial holdings, including 6,203,835 Class B shares through a revocable trust.

feed.title: Parker Increases Quarterly Cash Dividend 11% to $2.00 per Share
feed.summary: Parker Hannifin Corporation announced an 11% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $2.00 per share, payable June 5, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 8, 2026. This marks the 304th consecutive quarterly dividend and extends their track record of 70 consecutive years of increasing annual dividend payouts. The increase reflects the board's confidence in the company's consistent cash flow generation and long-term outlook.

feed.title: Is Digital Realty (DLR) 27.7% Overvalued After Q1 2026? EPS $0.4
feed.summary: Digital Realty (DLR) reported strong Q1 2026 results, with revenue up 16% year over year and GAAP EPS of $0.46, exceeding estimates. The company saw record interconnection and hyperscale leasing, leading to a raised 2026 Core FFO per share outlook. Despite this positive performance, GuruFocus assesses DLR as 27.7% overvalued based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Parker Hannifin Corporation Declares 11% Increase in Quarterly Cash Dividend to $2.00 per Share
feed.summary: Parker Hannifin Corporation has announced an 11% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $2.00 per share, marking its 304th consecutive quarterly payment and 70th consecutive fiscal year of annual dividend increases. The dividend is payable on June 5, 2026, to shareholders of record by May 8, reflecting the Board's confidence in the company's consistent cash flow and long-term outlook. CEO Jenny Parmentier highlighted the company's commitment to capital deployment through acquisitions, share repurchases, and returning value to shareholders.

feed.title: What's Going On With Tractor Supply Stock Today?
feed.summary: Tractor Supply (NASDAQ: TSCO) stock is down 8.85% today after the company reported first-quarter financial results that missed analyst expectations for earnings per share and sales. The decline is also influenced by a broader market downturn, with the S&P 500 and Consumer Discretionary sector experiencing slight losses. The company reaffirmed its 2026 GAAP EPS and sales guidance, but faces technical headwinds with its stock trading below key moving averages.

feed.title: AMG to Announce First Quarter Results on May 1, 2026
feed.summary: Affiliated Managers Group, Inc. (AMG) is scheduled to announce its first quarter 2026 financial and operating results on Friday, May 1, 2026. A conference call will be held on the same day at 8:00 a.m. Eastern time, hosted by President and CEO Jay C. Horgen and CFO Dava E. Ritchea, to discuss the results and potentially future expectations. Interested parties can access the live call, replay, and a presentation via phone or AMG’s investor relations website.

feed.title: Duke Energy's Robinson Nuclear Plant License Renewed for 20 Years
feed.summary: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission has renewed Duke Energy's Robinson Nuclear Plant operating license for an additional 20 years, allowing it to provide reliable power until 2050. This extension is crucial for maintaining regional electricity reliability and affordability, as the plant generates enough energy to power 570,000 homes in areas served by Duke Energy, which include 8.7 million customers across six states. The company is also implementing an energy modernization strategy to upgrade its electric grid and enhance generation resources, further solidifying its competitive edge and commitment to meeting growing energy needs.

feed.title: BNY Mellon High Yield Fund keeps monthly payout at $0.0175
feed.summary: The BNY Mellon High Yield Strategies Fund (NYSE: DHF) has declared a monthly cash dividend of $0.0175 per share, maintaining the same payout as the previous month. This dividend will be payable on May 21, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 7, 2026, with an ex-dividend date also on May 7, 2026. The declaration was made by the Fund's Board of Trustees on April 23, 2026.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial raises quarterly dividend by 6%
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial Inc. (NYSE:AMP) has increased its regular quarterly cash dividend by 6% to $1.70 per share, payable on May 22, 2026. This announcement follows a strong fourth quarter in 2025 where the company exceeded Wall Street expectations for earnings and revenue. Analysts have reiterated varied ratings, with Raymond James maintaining a "Strong Buy" and BMO Capital a "Market Perform."

feed.title: Transcript: Knowles Q1 2026 Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: Knowles (NYSE: KN) reported strong Q1 2026 results, with revenue up 16% year-over-year to $153 million and EPS up 50% year-over-year to $0.27, both exceeding guidance. The company anticipates continued strong organic growth and margin expansion throughout 2026, driven by robust demand in MedTech, defense, industrial, and electrification markets, and expects revenue growth to exceed its 4-6% target. Management highlighted strategic execution leveraging unique technologies for custom solutions, particularly in defense markets, leading to premium margins and confident future outlook.

feed.title: This Ridiculously Cheap Warren Buffett Stock Could Make You Richer
feed.summary: Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ: KHC) is presented as a "ridiculously cheap" investment opportunity despite being a past misstep for Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway. The article details Kraft Heinz's turnaround efforts, including a paused plan to split into two companies and a strategic investment in R&D and marketing to revive brand relevance. It suggests that the market may be undervaluing the company due to its complex history and ongoing transformation, making it a potential long-term value play for patient investors.

feed.title: Jefferies lowers Vistra Energy stock price target on valuation By Investing.com
feed.summary: Jefferies has lowered its price target for Vistra Energy (NYSE:VST) to $192 from $203, while maintaining a Buy rating, citing the company's valuation metrics as a compelling entry point. Despite the stock being down 30% since September 2025, it has delivered a 30.5% return over the past year. The firm believes the stock is not fully pricing in potential upside from future data center contracts, even with an overhang from ongoing PJM reform.

feed.title: Bank of New York Mellon issues $1.5 billion in senior notes due 2032 and 2037
feed.summary: Bank of New York Mellon has issued $1.5 billion in senior medium-term notes, comprising two equal tranches due in 2032 and 2037. This move, reported via an SEC filing, highlights the financial services giant's consistent financial stability, marked by 56 consecutive years of dividend payments. The issuance follows strong first-quarter 2026 results, where the company surpassed market expectations for both EPS and revenue.

feed.title: Huntington CEO Steve Steinour: Columbus still shielded (mostly) from recession
feed.summary: Huntington Bank CEO Steve Steinour announced that the bank plans to reduce the volume of new construction loans in its Texas operations. However, the bank will not implement similar reductions in Central Ohio, indicating a more stable economic outlook for the Columbus area. Steinour suggested that the Columbus community's embrace of AI could further enhance its long-term economic well-being.

feed.title: PSEG shareholders approve board nominees and key proposals at annual meeting
feed.summary: PSEG shareholders have re-elected all management nominees to the board of directors and approved executive compensation, increasing shares for the employee stock purchase plan, and ratifying Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor. Proposals to eliminate supermajority voting requirements did not pass. This comes amidst varied analyst ratings for the utility company, which has a market cap of $40 billion and a consistent dividend payment history.

feed.title: M. Susan Hardwick Joins Edison International, Southern California Edison Boards of Directors
feed.summary: M. Susan Hardwick has been elected as an independent director to the boards of Edison International and Southern California Edison, effective April 23, 2026. She brings over 35 years of leadership experience in regulated utilities and energy, including prior roles as CEO and CFO of American Water Works Company, Inc. Hardwick's expertise in utility operations, finance, risk management, and regulatory affairs is expected to significantly benefit the boards.

feed.title: Parker Increases Quarterly Cash Dividend 11% to $2.00 per Share
feed.summary: Parker Hannifin Corporation announced an 11% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $2.00 per share, marking the 304th consecutive quarterly dividend. This decision reflects the Board's confidence in the company's consistent cash flow generation and long-term outlook. Parker Hannifin has a 70-year track record of increasing its annual dividend, placing it among the top S&P 500 companies.

feed.title: Jabil declares $0.08 quarterly dividend payable June 2 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.08 per share, payable on June 2, 2026, marking 21 consecutive years of dividend payments. The company's stock appears overvalued according to InvestingPro analysis, despite strong Q2 fiscal 2026 results driven by AI and server demand, leading multiple analysts to raise their price targets. Jabil is also investing in community education through a significant donation to St. Petersburg College.

feed.title: Charles Schwab declares $0.32 quarterly dividend per share By Investing.com
feed.summary: The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE:SCHW) announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.32 per common share, payable on May 22, 2026, to stockholders of record as of May 8, 2026, marking 38 consecutive years of dividend payments. The company also declared dividends on six series of preferred stock. Schwab reported 39.1 million active brokerage accounts and $11.77 trillion in client assets, with its stock appearing undervalued relative to its Fair Value by InvestingPro.

feed.title: Trees are good for the earth and help lower energy bills
feed.summary: Xcel Energy encourages customers in Texas and New Mexico to strategically plant trees to help manage energy use and lower bills, especially during summer months. Properly placed trees can provide shade, block wind, and reduce the need for air conditioning, leading to energy bill savings. The company also advises calling 811 before digging to ensure utility line safety and provides resources for optimal tree planting and energy-saving tips.

feed.title: Seagate Technology (NASDAQ:STX) Trading 1.3% Higher After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Seagate Technology (NASDAQ:STX) saw its share price increase by 1.3% after Barclays upgraded the stock from an "equal weight" to an "overweight" rating and significantly raised its price target to $625.00 from $425.00. This upgrade, alongside other recent analyst target increases, reflects positive sentiment driven by Seagate's strong Q2 earnings, revenue growth, and expansion into high-capacity products for AI and other demanding workflows. However, the article also notes potential risks such as heavy insider selling, a high P/E ratio, and volatility associated with new leveraged ETFs.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE:EW) Releases Q2 2026 Earnings Guidance
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE:EW) has updated its Q2 2026 earnings guidance, projecting EPS between $0.700 and $0.760 and revenue of $1.7 billion, while also raising its full-year 2026 EPS guidance to $2.95-$3.05. Despite the positive guidance, the stock traded down to $79.69, and company insiders have sold over $7 million worth of shares in the past 90 days. Analysts currently maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock with an average price target of $95.74.

feed.title: VeriSign (NASDAQ:VRSN) Posts Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.04 EPS
feed.summary: VeriSign (NASDAQ:VRSN) announced quarterly earnings results, reporting $2.34 EPS, which surpassed analysts' consensus estimates of $2.30 by $0.04. The information services provider also posted revenues of $428.9 million, exceeding the estimated $424.5 million. Following the report, the stock traded up $7.12 to $276.95, and the company increased its quarterly dividend to $0.81 per share.

feed.title: LSI Industries Inc. (NASDAQ:LYTS) Plans $0.05 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: LSI Industries Inc. (NASDAQ:LYTS) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.05 per share, payable on May 12th to shareholders of record on May 4th. This equates to an annual dividend of $0.20, offering a yield of approximately 0.9%. The company's dividend is well-covered, with a payout ratio of 18.7% and future analyst forecasts suggesting an even lower payout of 15.4%.

feed.title: Frontier AI and the Future of Defense: Your Top Questions Answered
feed.summary: This article addresses the impact of "frontier AI" on cybersecurity and defense, highlighting the challenges posed by its rapid weaponization potential despite its innovative capabilities. It answers ten common questions from security leaders, emphasizing the need for machine-speed defense, AI-driven platforms, and a proactive shift-left strategy to counter autonomous threats. The article also introduces Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 Frontier AI Defense service, aimed at helping organizations identify attack paths before they are exploited.

feed.title: T-Mobile increases shareholder return program by $3.6 billion
feed.summary: T-Mobile US, Inc. has announced a significant increase of $3.6 billion to its 2026 Shareholder Return Program, bringing the total authorized returns to $18.2 billion through the end of 2026. This program encompasses both share repurchases and cash dividends, funded by available cash and debt issuances. The enhancement reflects management's aggressive share buyback strategy and comes amidst speculation of a full combination with Deutsche Telekom, which is currently in preliminary discussions.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines declares quarterly dividend of $0.1875 per share
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines announced a quarterly dividend of $0.1875 per share, payable June 4, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 14, 2026. The airline has demonstrated a commitment to shareholder returns, having raised its dividend for three consecutive years with a 25% growth over the past year. This announcement comes amidst discussions of increased jet fuel costs due to geopolitical conflicts and varying analyst outlooks on airline stocks.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (NYSE:HWM) to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.12
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (NYSE:HWM) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share, payable on May 26th to shareholders of record on May 8th. The company recently reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings of $1.05 EPS and $2.17 billion in revenue, also providing strong guidance for Q1 and FY 2026. Analysts currently rate Howmet Aerospace as a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $266.89.

feed.title: Record 2025 results and key 2026 proxy votes at Limbach (NASDAQ: LMB)
feed.summary: Limbach Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: LMB) has announced record financial results for 2025, with revenue up 24.7% to $646.8 million and Adjusted EBITDA increasing 28.4% to $81.8 million. The company's proxy statement outlines key votes for its upcoming June 9, 2026 annual meeting, including the election of three Class A directors and advisory votes on executive compensation. Additionally, Limbach highlights the success of its Owner Direct Relationships strategy, which accounted for approximately 75% of total revenue, and the strategic acquisition of Pioneer Power.

feed.title: Jefferson Capital (JCAP) lifts revolving credit commitments to $1,150,000,000
feed.summary: Jefferson Capital, Inc. (JCAP) has increased its aggregate revolving credit commitments by $150,000,000, bringing the total to $1,150,000,000 through Amendment No. 8 to its Credit Agreement. This amendment also raises the maximum cap on future incremental increases to $1,425,000,000, allowing for additional potential increases of up to $275,000,000. Aside from these changes to the revolving credit capacity, the underlying Credit Agreement remains substantially the same.

feed.title: Duke Energy’s Robinson Nuclear Plant approved to operate until 2050
feed.summary: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission has renewed the operating license for Duke Energy's Robinson Nuclear Plant, allowing it to continue operations until 2050. This 20-year extension supports grid reliability, energy affordability, and is expected to result in cost savings for customers by utilizing existing generation capacity. The plant is a significant power source for 570,000 homes and contributes substantially to Duke Energy's Carolina fleet.

feed.title: Nexus (NYSE: LSF) funds $48M Terrasoul deal with $60M preferred
feed.summary: Nexus-affiliated investors now hold a 73.8% stake in Laird Superfood, Inc. after the company funded its $48 million acquisition of Terrasoul Superfoods, LLC with $60 million in preferred stock. This financing involved the issuance of 60,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock to Nexus Special Situations vehicles, convertible into common stock at $3.57 per share. The transaction, detailed in a SCHEDULE 13D/A filing, underscores Nexus's majority control over Laird Superfood on an as-converted basis.

feed.title: Affiliate files Form 144 to sell restricted shares (STLD)
feed.summary: An affiliate of Steel Dynamics Inc. (STLD) has filed a Form 144 notice, indicating a proposed sale of common stock acquired through restricted stock awards. The filing specifies three award lots totaling 1,717, 1,825, and 1,458 shares, with grant dates ranging from 2022 to 2024. This action is a notice of intent to sell rather than a completed transaction, with specific details like sale prices and brokers not yet disclosed.
